assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Systematic force-upgrade to 1.31 (next supported minor version)' and 'Control plane upgraded first, then all node pools' for Regular channel clusters

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Extended support continues until up to 24 months from 1.30's original GA date' and provides specific timeline showing Extended EoS at ~May 2026 vs Regular at ~May 2025

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: States 'Same systematic force-upgrade as Regular channel' and specifically mentions 'No auto-upgrade per-nodepool settings are ignored during EoS enforcement'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions 'No way to prevent this except temporary no upgrades exclusion (30-day max)' and 'Only the 30-day no upgrades exclusion can temporarily defer'

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Provides detailed 'Proactive manual upgrade (Recommended)' section with specific commands and emphasizes upgrading before forced upgrades occur

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Does not mention Cloud Logging notifications for EoS planning - this specific monitoring mechanism is not discussed

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Only mentions checking deprecated API usage via kubectl metrics command, but does not specifically reference the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly mention 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions and associated security/reliability risks
